mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate increases risk-free rate and discount rates, reducing present value of future corporate cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negatively correlated with FEDFUNDS level (Spearman rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment rate above a threshold signals weakening labor demand and impending earnings recession, causing negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated contraction in both final demand and goods output, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO do not produce lower S&P 500 20-day returns than either proxy alone (interaction term p-value > 0.05 in panel regression over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its recent trend signals reduced corporate operating leverage and revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 1.0% over 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of triggered months over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below a trailing peak indicates weakening construction activity and durables demand, dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines > 10% from 12-month peak are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect robust consumer spending that supports corporate top-line growth and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS monthly changes (Spearman rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating inflation during a restrictive monetary policy regime signals impending cessation of rate hikes, reducing terminal rate uncertainty and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL decelerates, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate tight labor markets translating into wage income and consumption, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth > 300k in 3 months is followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a rolling 2-year window.
